corePrinciple: Props should clarify Otis as a threshold keeper without becoming a second character, hiding his slight belly, or forcing fragile hand anatomy.

propBudget.mobileReadRule: At 64 px tall, only one prop should be instantly readable; all other prop details must collapse into quiet costume accents.
propBudget.silhouetteRule: Props stay inside the shoulder-to-elbow envelope or below the belt line unless the pose specifically needs a greeting card extension.

canonicalProps.storyFunction: Threshold signal and concierge ritual; strongest for alert and working poses.
canonicalProps.formLanguage: Small low dome, squat black felt base, tiny plunger, warm aged brass with softened highlights.
canonicalProps.scale: Roughly palm-width, never larger than Otis's hand span.
safeUsage: Resting on a narrow half-moon threshold ledge near his hip.
Pressed with one extended index finger while the other hand remains open.
Carried only on a small flat tray, never pinched or gripped around the dome.
avoid: Do not make it shiny gold.
Do not put fingers wrapped around the plunger.
Do not let it float beside him without contact.
Do not add sound lines, sparkles, or cartoon effects.

canonicalProps.storyFunction: Permission, access, and next-step invitation; strongest for greeting and talking poses.
canonicalProps.formLanguage: Rounded ivory card, one brass edge stripe, simple embossed tower mark, no readable text.
canonicalProps.scale: Credit-card scale, oversized by only 5-10 percent for mobile readability.
safeUsage: Pinched flat between thumb and index finger with the card face visible.
Tucked halfway into breast pocket or ledger band when not active.
Held away from the face and beard so it does not clutter the expression.
avoid: No tiny text, QR codes, barcodes, or UI labels.
No twisted fingers threading through a lanyard.

canonicalProps.storyFunction: Human memory, old-world concierge reliability, and lived-in warmth; strongest for listening and thinking poses.
canonicalProps.formLanguage: Soft rectangular folio, rounded worn corners, cream page edge, muted brass clasp.
canonicalProps.scale: Small notebook to tablet-folio scale; must not cover the belly curve.
safeUsage: Closed and tucked under one forearm.
Cradled against the side ribs with fingers mostly behind the edge.
Open only as a simple two-page silhouette with no readable writing.
avoid: No anatomically complex hand positions across the spine.

canonicalProps.storyFunction: Modern internship command-center operator without losing concierge warmth; strongest for working poses.
canonicalProps.formLanguage: Thin dark slate screen inside oxblood leather folio, soft reflection, one brass corner guard.
canonicalProps.scale: Slightly smaller than a letter sheet, tucked low enough to preserve Otis's belly silhouette.
safeUsage: Held like a serving tray against the forearm.
Resting on the half-moon ledge beside the bell.
Screen mostly dark, with abstract warm blocks rather than legible UI.
avoid: No bright dashboard UI competing with Otis's face.
No two-handed typing pose unless generated as a separate high-risk probe.

outfitIntegration.propAccent: Aged brass and oxblood leather feel native; keycard can match ivory shirt highlights.
Keep brass less shiny so the Santa warmth stays human, not costume-store festive.
Tablet folio is allowed, but ledger texture should keep the lived-in threshold-keeper read.

stressFindings: A bell is story-rich but dangerous as a handheld object; treat it as ledge/tray supported in most poses.
A keycard is readable and hand-safe only if it is flat, oversized slightly, and not attached to keys.
A ledger/tablet can accidentally erase the approved slight belly; keep it side-cradled or low.
More than one active prop per sprite makes Otis feel like inventory UI rather than a human concierge.
